title: 3. Responsibilities 职责 > 3.4 RA/QA 法规部/质量部
text_content: Provided input to traceability evaluation based upon requirements from both domestic and international regulatory authorities and approved this Plan. 根据国内和国际监管机构的要求，为可追溯性评估提供输入，并批准本计划。 4. Traceability Evaluation 可追溯性评价 4.1 This traceability plan was developed for HAL4.0 production.  As there are 3 Key types of Components, or sub-assemblies of the device, three separate tables were developed. 该可追溯性计划专门为HAL4.0 生产而制定。由于设备的部件或子组件主要分为三种类型，因此创建 了三份独立的表格。 4.2 Appendix C describes the buy level components which include Off-the-Shelf components, customized parts, buy level FRU components, Critical Components. Critical components are identified in D52223-HAL4.0 Process Controls and Critical components List and D61200- HAL4.0 Design Failure Modes and Effects Analysis 附录C 对包括现成部件、定制部件、采购的FRU 部件、关键部件在内的采购部件进行了介绍。 D52223-HAL4.0 过程控制和关键部件清单，以及D61200-HAL4.0 设计失效模式及影响分析报告对关 键部件进行了界定。 4.3 Appendix D describes the in-house make level Components, in house make FRU components included.

sub_query: document outlining traceability of requirements to design artifacts
title: 2. Requirements 需求
text_content: Per procedure INC02, all medical devices need to have a Traceability Plan in place which describes the traceability requirements for the components of the device.  Any future changes to the product require that this plan be reviewed and updated to comply with the product’s related Critical To Safety and Critical To Reliability lists.  The approved Traceability Plan shall be stored in the Device Master Record (DMR). 根据INC02 程序，所有医疗设备都需要有可追溯性计划，该计划描述了设备组件的可追溯性要求。 未来 对产品的任何更改都要求对该计划进行审查和更新，以符合产品相关的关键安全和关键可靠性列表。 经 批准的追溯计划应存储在设备主记录(DMR)中.

text_content: 2.1. Validation means confirmation by examination and provision of objective evidence that the particular requirements for a specific intended use can be consistently fulfilled. 21CFR Part 820.3 (z) 2.2. Process validation means establishing by objective evidence that a process consistently produces a result or product meeting its predetermined specifications. 21CFR Part 820.3 (z) (1) 2.3. Design validation means establishing by objective evidence that device specifications conform to user needs and intended use(s). 21CFR Part 820.3 (z) (2) 2.4. Verification means confirmation by examination and provision of objective evidence that specified requirements have been fulfilled. 21CFR Part 820.3 (z) (2) (aa) 2.5. Test Case: A document specifying inputs, predicted results, and a set of execution conditions for a test item (IEEE 829 Standard) 2.6. Test Report provides the results of executed protocol in detail and describes if acceptance criteria were met.

title: 2. Definitions
text_content: 2.1. Validation means confirmation by examination and provision of objective evidence that the particular requirements for a specific intended use can be consistently fulfilled. 21CFR Part 820.3 (z) 2.2. Process validation means establishing by objective evidence that a process consistently produces a result or product meeting its predetermined specifications. 21CFR Part 820.3 (z) (1) 2.3. Design validation means establishing by objective evidence that device specifications conform to user needs and intended use(s). 21CFR Part 820.3 (z) (2) 2.4. Verification means confirmation by examination and provision of objective evidence that specified requirements have been fulfilled. 21CFR Part 820.3 (z) (2) (aa) 2.5. Test Protocol: A detailed set of instructions/plan for conduct of verification and validation in the current context. 2.6. source: D65245 HAL 4.0 Packaging Validation Protocol and Report Rev 04.docx.pdf
title: 10. Summary of Test Results
text_content: The packages maintained integrity and the devices passed visual and functionality testing, therefore the packaging is validated and approved for production usage. Full test results are attached. The 3-Phase Isolation Transformer crate showed minor damage to wooden support blocks that surround the bracket at the base of the transformer. There was no damage to the transformer and functionality was maintained. Given the extreme nature of the testing which is outside what is expected to be seen in our real-world distribution environment, it is acceptable and expected that there would be minor damage. The packaging is expected to provide sacrificial protection to the product. The transformer did not move such that it would come in contact with the walls of the crate or out of the straps that secure it, therefore the wooden support blocks performed their function, no changes are required to the design, and the packaging is considered validated. Figure 1 shows the damage to the wooden blocks.
